text: DNA strand breaks activate a series of DNA damage response (DDR) pathways that together determine whether damaged cells go through cell cycle arrest, repair, or cell death. When the levels of DNA damage exceed the DNA repair capacity, DDR signaling shifts the cellular response toward elimination of damaged cells by various forms of cell death. Thus, DNA strand breaks are potent inducers of programmed cell death (i.e., apoptosis) when damage is irreparable or sustained.
